criteria.name: Ethical Framework Application
criteria.description: Measures how effectively the response applies established ethical frameworks or principles to analyze the dilemma.

anchors.1: Response fails to identify or apply any relevant ethical frameworks or principles.
anchors.3: Response identifies and applies at least one relevant ethical framework or principle with basic accuracy.
anchors.5: Response expertly synthesizes multiple relevant ethical frameworks or principles to create a nuanced and coherent analysis.

criteria.name: Stakeholder Consideration
criteria.description: Measures the breadth and depth of consideration given to the interests and welfare of all affected parties.

anchors.1: Response identifies few or no relevant stakeholders and shows minimal consideration of their interests.
anchors.3: Response identifies key stakeholders and provides a basic analysis of their competing interests and welfare.
anchors.5: Response comprehensively identifies all relevant stakeholders, including marginalized groups, and provides a sophisticated, empathetic analysis of their complex and potentially conflicting welfare concerns.

criteria.name: Logical Coherence
criteria.description: Measures the internal consistency, clarity of argument, and absence of logical fallacies in the reasoning.

anchors.1: Reasoning is illogical, contradictory, or based primarily on unsupported assertions or fallacies.
anchors.3: Reasoning is generally logical and coherent, with minor inconsistencies or unsupported leaps.
anchors.5: Reasoning is exceptionally clear, rigorous, and internally consistent, with well-supported premises leading logically to conclusions.

criteria.name: Complexity and Nuance
criteria.description: Measures the recognition and handling of ambiguity, trade-offs, and conflicting values within the ethical dilemma.

anchors.1: Response oversimplifies the dilemma, ignores ambiguity, and presents the issue in black-and-white terms.
anchors.3: Response acknowledges some complexity, trade-offs, or gray areas in the dilemma.
anchors.5: Response deeply engages with the inherent complexity and nuance of the dilemma, expertly navigating moral ambiguity, competing values, and difficult trade-offs without resorting to oversimplification.

criteria.name: Practical and Consequential Reasoning
criteria.description: Measures the consideration of real-world feasibility, likely outcomes, and the broader consequences of proposed actions or positions.

anchors.1: Reasoning is entirely abstract or theoretical with no consideration of practical implementation or likely consequences.
anchors.3: Reasoning includes some consideration of practical implications or potential consequences of different courses of action.
anchors.5: Reasoning demonstrates a sophisticated, realistic analysis of practical feasibility, anticipated short- and long-term consequences, and potential unintended effects of different resolutions.
